concerns.text: FR-004 mandates 'paired statistical significance testing (Wilcoxon signed-rank or t-test)' for methods trained independently. This is methodologically unverifiable as a success criterion because the 'paired' condition cannot be met by independent model training (GPR vs NN). The spec must define a verifiable pairing mechanism (e.g., identical random seeds for all models on the same test split) or switch to an unpaired test (Mann-Whitney U) to be testable.

Constitution Principle VII mandates 'paired statistical tests' for performance comparisons. This contradicts the statistical reality of independent model training described in the methodology. A test cannot verify 'paired' results if the data generation process (independent training) does not produce paired samples. This creates an untestable requirement where the system cannot satisfy the 'paired' constraint without violating the 'independent training' constraint.

FR-002 lists 'GPR as a standalone model replacing the baseline' but the methodology sketch implies applying UQ techniques 'to the same baseline predictor'. The functional requirement is ambiguous: is GPR a replacement or an add-on? This ambiguity makes it difficult to verify if the implementation matches the spec without guessing the intended architecture.
